title: Sleep difficulty
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: insomnia
assessment.rationale: Exact phrase located in inspected full source.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Insomnia
source.population: Adults with difficulty initiating or maintaining sleep or early waking with daytime impact.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth insomnia history, red flags, causes, and clinician-led management; fixed follow-up intervals and autonomous medication choices are not supported.

exact_location.heading: Sections 5–7 — sleep history and daytime consequences

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports sleep initiation or maintenance difficulty, early waking, non-restorative sleep, adequate opportunity, fatigue, concentration, mood, irritability, daytime sleepiness, motivation, duration, stress, and functional impact.

assessment.text: Supports sleep initiation or maintenance difficulty, early waking, non-restorative sleep, adequate opportunity, fatigue, concentration, mood, irritability, daytime sleepiness, motivation, duration, stress, and functional impact.

exact_location.heading: Sections 5–7 — sleep history and daytime consequences

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports sleep initiation or maintenance difficulty, early waking, non-restorative sleep, adequate opportunity, fatigue, concentration, mood, irritability, daytime sleepiness, motivation, duration, stress, and functional impact.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Depression
source.population: Adults presenting with suspected or established depressive symptoms; emergency, severe, psychotic, bipolar, or safeguarding presentations require escalation.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth depression documentation and risk screening; treatment, diagnosis, and local emergency pathways remain clinician-led.

exact_location.heading: Section 7 — risk, mental-status examination, and selected investigations

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician assessment of suicide, self-harm, harm or neglect, protective and support factors, mental status, cognition, insight, substance use, medical contributors, medication history, thyroid testing, and selected blood testing when indicated.

assessment.text: Lists sleep attacks, apnoea or gasping, unstable cardiopulmonary disease, stroke, injury during sleep, sleepwalking, excessive daytime sleepiness, substance misuse, depression, anxiety, and significant functional impact; supports sleep-diary and targeted evaluation.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Insomnia
source.population: Adults with difficulty initiating or maintaining sleep or early waking with daytime impact.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth insomnia history, red flags, causes, and clinician-led management; fixed follow-up intervals and autonomous medication choices are not supported.

exact_location.heading: Section 7 — red flags and evaluation

source.evidence_paraphrase: Lists sleep attacks, apnoea or gasping, unstable cardiopulmonary disease, stroke, injury during sleep, sleepwalking, excessive daytime sleepiness, substance misuse, depression, anxiety, and significant functional impact; supports sleep-diary and targeted evaluation.

assessment.text: Supports caffeine, nicotine, medication, substance, sleep-apnoea, restless-legs, pain, withdrawal, thyroid, dyspnoea, mood, anxiety, life-stress, bedtime-worry, environmental, and sleep-habit review plus selected tests when clinically indicated.
assessment.rationale: Supports mapped caffeine, medication, substance, thyroid, blood-test, and contributing-condition review.

assessment.text: Supports clinician-documented discussion of sleep routine or hygiene, lifestyle context, psychological or medication plans, reassessment, questions, monitoring, and referral without autonomous advice or doses.

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician-documented discussion of sleep routine or hygiene, lifestyle context, psychological or medication plans, reassessment, questions, monitoring, and referral without autonomous advice or doses.

assessment.text: Supports clinician assessment of suicide, self-harm, harm or neglect, protective and support factors, mental status, cognition, insight, substance use, medical contributors, medication history, thyroid testing, and selected blood testing when indicated.
assessment.rationale: Supports mapped mental-status and suicide or self-harm risk documentation when depression is assessed as a comorbidity.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Depression
source.population: Adults presenting with suspected or established depressive symptoms; emergency, severe, psychotic, bipolar, or safeguarding presentations require escalation.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth depression documentation and risk screening; treatment, diagnosis, and local emergency pathways remain clinician-led.

exact_location.heading: Section 7 — risk, mental-status examination, and selected investigations

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician assessment of suicide, self-harm, harm or neglect, protective and support factors, mental status, cognition, insight, substance use, medical contributors, medication history, thyroid testing, and selected blood testing when indicated.

rewrites.final_wording: Lists sleep attacks, apnoea or gasping, unstable cardiopulmonary disease, stroke, injury during sleep, sleepwalking, excessive daytime sleepiness, substance misuse, depression, anxiety, and significant functional impact; supports sleep-diary and targeted evaluation.
